metadata.title: The chickadees found the frozen cattails
metadata.curiosityNote: Black-capped chickadees get chattier when noise and activity pick up around a wetland.

story.briefTemplate: At a Minnesota wetland, Seraphina set a tiny recorder near rustling cattails. Before lunch it caught {quantity:morning_chickadee_calls}. In the afternoon, extra noise from the bustling nearby neighborhood caused the chickadees to get more vocal, and the recorder caught {quantity:afternoon_chickadee_calls}. It also caught {quantity:nuthatch_taps} from a nuthatch on a dead birch.
story.causalEvent: Neighborhood noise made the chickadees more vocal in the afternoon.
story.closingNoteTemplate: The recorder log closed with {quantity:total_bird_sounds} in all.

label.child: Chickadee calls in the noisy afternoon
All bird sounds the recorder caught

steps.prompt: How many chickadee calls did the recorder catch altogether?
steps.reasoningPrompt: The morning calls and afternoon calls are both chickadee calls. How do they make the chickadee total?

backwardCheck.prompt: Take the afternoon calls back off. Do you land on the morning count?

steps.prompt: How many bird sounds did the recorder catch altogether?
steps.reasoningPrompt: The chickadee calls and nuthatch taps are both sounds in the recorder log. How do they make the total?

backwardCheck.prompt: Take the nuthatch taps back out. Do you reach the chickadee calls?

operatorExperiments.alternateWorldTemplate: This matches the story: the noisy afternoon added {quantity:afternoon_chickadee_calls} to the morning count.
Subtracting would fit a different story where the afternoon lost calls instead of gaining them.
This would fit a different question: if there were {value:afternoon_chickadee_calls} stretches like {label:morning_chickadee_calls.lowercase}, how many would there be?
This would fit a different question about sharing {label:morning_chickadee_calls.lowercase} into equal groups.
This matches the story: {label:total_chickadee_calls.lowercase} and {label:nuthatch_taps.lowercase} combine into all the bird sounds.
Subtracting would fit a different story where {quantity:nuthatch_taps} were removed instead of added.
This would fit a different question: if there were {value:nuthatch_taps} groups like {label:total_chickadee_calls.lowercase}, how many would there be?
This would fit a different question about sharing {label:total_chickadee_calls.lowercase} into equal groups.

recap.headline: Why the recorder log reached {quantity:total_bird_sounds}
causalChain: Neighborhood noise made the chickadees more vocal, adding {quantity:afternoon_chickadee_calls}.

dataQuestion.prompt: What does {quantity:afternoon_chickadee_calls} represent in the chickadee model?
dataQuestion.correctFeedback: Right. {quantity:afternoon_chickadee_calls} is the noisy-afternoon count added to the morning calls.
dataQuestion.incorrectFeedback: That amount is the noisy-afternoon count: {quantity:afternoon_chickadee_calls}.
